duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: closeMarket arithmetic underflow when availableLiquidity -= normalizedAmountPaid uses raw subtraction instead of satSub, caused by rounding mismatch between scaleAmount (rayDiv, rounds to nearest) and mulDiv (rounds down) in _applyWithdrawalBatchPayment when scaleFactor >= 2*RAY
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same root cause: raw subtraction in closeMarket's unpaid batch loop where normalizedAmountPaid exceeds availableLiquidity due to scaleAmount/mulDiv rounding mismatch. TECH-H001 has the strongest evidence (direct code comparison with satSub usage in repayAndProcessUnpaidWithdrawalBatches). ECON-H012 and OPS-H002 combine this with the dust issue but primarily focus on this underflow.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: closeMarket reverts due to non-zero rounding dust in scaledPendingWithdrawals after processing all batches, because scaleAmount(normalizeAmount(x)) < x leaves irrecoverable 1-wei dust
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same failure mode: the strict scaledPendingWithdrawals != 0 check fails due to lossy integer round-trip through scaleAmount/normalizeAmount, even when sufficient normalized liquidity exists. TECH-H002 has more precise technical analysis of the round-trip loss.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: closeMarket loop repeatedly processes the same first unpaid batch without queue progress because _processUnpaidWithdrawalBatch always reads first() and only removes on full payment
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the identical logic flaw: closeMarket's for-loop iterates numBatches times but always processes first() from the unpaidBatches queue. If the first batch can't be fully paid, it stays at front and the loop wastes gas. TECH-H003 has the clearest code comparison with repayAndProcessUnpaidWithdrawalBatches.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Unbounded unpaidBatches array growth enables block gas limit DoS on closeMarket via repeated small withdrawal queuing with short withdrawalBatchDuration
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same attack: a lender queues many tiny withdrawals that create distinct expired unpaid batches when withdrawalBatchDuration is short, bloating the array so closeMarket exceeds block gas limit. ECON-H006 has the most detailed attack economics and preconditions analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: onSetAnnualInterestAndReserveRatioBips always reverts due to Yul evaluation order: or(lt(returndatasize(),0x40), iszero(call(...))) evaluates returndatasize() before the call executes, reading stale 32-byte returndata from prior balanceOf
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the identical assembly evaluation order bug in HooksConfig.sol where returndatasize() reflects the previous balanceOf call (32 bytes), making lt(32,64)=true and or(true,X)=true, causing unconditional revert. ECON-H001 has the most comprehensive impact assessment including economics and victim analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: onBorrow hook assembly uses RepayHook_Base_Size instead of BorrowHook_Base_Size for calldata sizing (latent copy-paste error, both currently equal 0x0224)
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All identify the same copy-paste error in HooksConfig.sol where onBorrow uses RepayHook_Base_Size. Both constants are 0x0224 so no current impact. TECH-H005 has the most precise proof_of_signal with exact line reference.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: pushProtocolFeeBipsUpdates reverts entire batch if any single market is closed or already has the target fee, due to assembly call with no error handling
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same architectural flaw: HooksFactory.pushProtocolFeeBipsUpdates uses assembly call that reverts entire transaction if any market.setProtocolFeeBips fails (closed market or unchanged fee). TECH-H006 has HIGH severity rating and clear code evidence. Note: TECH2-H003 here refers to the 'pushProtocolFeeBipsUpdates DoS via Closed Market' entry.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: withdrawableProtocolFees arithmetic underflow (panic) when totalAssets < normalizedUnclaimedWithdrawals during severe delinquency, blocking collectFees
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same unchecked subtraction in MarketState.withdrawableProtocolFees where totalAssets - normalizedUnclaimedWithdrawals panics when totalAssets is lower. TECH-H007 has the clearest code reference and appropriate severity.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Fee-on-transfer token inflates internal accounting because deposit/repay credit scaledAmount based on input amount without verifying actual received balance delta
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same missing balance-delta check after safeTransferFrom in _depositUpTo and repay functions. The market credits the full input amount but may receive less with fee-on-transfer tokens, leading to insolvency. TECH-H010 has the most structured analysis with clear config_dependent marking and deployment assumptions.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: executeWithdrawal division-by-zero revert when called with expiry that has no batch (batch.scaledTotalAmount == 0), as mulDiv has no explicit zero-divisor guard
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same missing batch-existence validation in _executeWithdrawal where mulDiv(batch.normalizedAmountPaid, status.scaledAmount, batch.scaledTotalAmount) reverts on division by zero for nonexistent batches. TECH-H012 was identified first with clear proof_of_signal. Note: TECH2-H003 here refers to the 'Withdrawal execution can revert via division-by-zero' entry.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Hooks contract can silently override borrower's requested annualInterestBips and reserveRatioBips in setAnnualInterestAndReserveRatioBips, only bounded by > BIP check
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe hooks overriding borrower parameters via onSetAnnualInterestAndReserveRatioBips return values. ECON-H010 provides broader analysis including the stale-state validation angle and more detailed proof_of_signal.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Pro-rata withdrawal rounding dust from mulDiv rounds-down permanently locks small amounts in normalizedUnclaimedWithdrawals, accumulating over market lifetime
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same rounding dust accumulation in _executeWithdrawal where mulDiv rounds down for each user's share, leaving irrecoverable dust in normalizedUnclaimedWithdrawals. ECON-H017 has the more comprehensive impact analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: TransientBytesArray decoding can revert with Panic_InvalidStorageByteArray during market deployment if transient storage encoding is corrupted or malformed
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same risk: TransientBytesArray's extractByteArrayLength enforces strict encoding invariants and panics on mismatch, potentially bricking market deployment via getMarketParameters(). OPS-H014 covers the broader deployment flow context.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: HooksFactory template deployment copies code with extcodecopy offset 1 and size-1, assuming stored-initcode format; malformed template produces invalid initcode
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same code pattern: _deployHooksInstance uses extcodesize(template)-1 and extcodecopy starting at offset 1, assuming a specific template bytecode format. A misregistered template causes deployment failures. OPS-H015 has broader coverage of the operational impact.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: normalizedUnclaimedWithdrawals progressively over-reserved via scaleAmount rounding in _applyWithdrawalBatchPayment where normalizedAmountPaid exceeds input availableLiquidity
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same rounding error in _applyWithdrawalBatchPayment where scaleAmount rounds up and mulDiv rounds down, causing normalizedAmountPaid > availableLiquidity. This accumulates over time inflating normalizedUnclaimedWithdrawals. ECON2-H009 has the most detailed analysis of ongoing accumulation and impact on borrowableAssets and collectFees.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: transferFrom reduces allowance by user-specified amount but actual value transferred is normalizeAmount(scaleAmount(amount)) which may differ due to rounding
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same allowance/transfer rounding mismatch in transferFrom where allowance decreases by `amount` but actual transfer uses scaleAmount(amount). ECON2-H010 has more detailed attack economics analysis.
